displayTitle: Hot Steel Forgets, Hard Steel Holds

beats.narration: Magnetize two steels with the same shot, and you can get wildly different fields. Heat and hardness decide how the steel answers.

payload.title: STEEL PUSHES BACK
payload.sub: What heat and hardness do to your magnetic field.

beats.narration: Heat works against you. As steel heats, permeability falls, and at the Curie point, about 770 degrees Celsius for iron, ferromagnetism vanishes entirely.

payload.heading: Magnetism dies at the Curie point
payload.note: ABOVE 770 °C — NO MT UNTIL IT COOLS

beats.narration: Hardness pulls the other way. Hard, heat-treated steel draws a wide hysteresis loop and holds magnetism stubbornly. Soft annealed steel lets go almost instantly.

payload.heading: Hard steel holds, soft steel lets go
payload.note: WIDE LOOP HOLDS · NARROW LOOP LETS GO

beats.narration: Retention picks your method. Hard, retentive parts keep a useful field — the residual method works. Soft steel demands the continuous method.

chips.s: Keeps a field after the shot

beats.narration: Lock it in: hot steel forgets, hard steel holds. Above the Curie point, no test — a wide loop earns the residual method.

payload.sub: No MT above the Curie point — retention picks the method.
